method: Black-box e2e against the built CLI (`node dist/index.js`). HOME redirected to a temp sandbox so the user's real config is never touched. Each scenario writes a known config state, runs the CLI, and captures stdout+stderr+exit code. Criteria are graded against observed CLI behaviour; criteria that cannot be reached without a live pi provider are marked unit_only and reference the test that proves them.

scenarios.title: Fresh sandbox, no config — kode-review --doctor
scenarios.exit_meaning: Non-zero is correct: pi has no provider configured. Doctor itself ran end-to-end.
observed: Doctor ran without triggering migration prompt.
Reported: pi installed, gh installed (unauth), glab missing, docker running.
Wrote fresh default config to disk on first read.
On-disk config contains zero legacy keys (no `provider`, `model`, `variant`, `antigravity`).

scenarios.title: Fresh sandbox — kode-review --show-config
observed: Output prints config sections; reports `Owned by pi (https://pi.dev). Run pi --list-models to inspect.` for Model & Auth.
grep counts: provider=0, variant=0, antigravity=0, sk-…=0, ghp_/gho_/glpat-=0.

scenarios.title: Legacy config, no TTY — non-doctor command should refuse without --migrate-yes
scenarios.setup: Wrote legacy config containing `provider`, `model`, `variant`, `antigravity`, `indexer.composeProject=legacy-test-project`, plus a watch-state file.
observed: Migration warning printed (4 bullet items including config path, watch dir, indexer Docker project).
Detected non-TTY: `Cannot prompt for confirmation: stdin is not a TTY.`
Suggested `--migrate-yes` / `KODE_REVIEW_MIGRATE_YES=1`.
Exited with `Aborted. No changes made.`
Post-run: legacy `provider` key STILL present, watch state file STILL present (proves abort = zero side effects).

scenarios.title: Legacy config — kode-review --doctor
observed: Doctor ran end-to-end; migration gate did NOT fire (matches AC-09 first criterion).
Reported pi/git/docker/gh/glab and indexer-containers status.
Reported `Configuration: Valid and complete` — did NOT flag the legacy schema (see AC-09 last criterion: gap).

scenarios.title: Legacy config — KODE_REVIEW_MIGRATE_YES=1 forces migration
observed: Migration banner printed.
Spinner: `Tearing down indexer Docker project "legacy-test-project"…` — proves composeProject was read from legacy config BEFORE wipe.
Printed `✔ Migration complete.` followed by `Run kode-review --setup to set up v1.0.`
Post-migration config: zero legacy keys, `composeProject` reset to default `kode-review-indexer`, `onboardingComplete=false`.
Watch dir `~/.config/kode-review-watch/` removed entirely.
Note: after migration the originally-requested --show-config did NOT execute; CLI exits and asks the user to re-run with --setup. Acceptable v1.0 UX, just observed.

scenarios.title: Legacy config — --scope local (review path) refused without --migrate-yes
observed: Same migration-required block as S3.
Confirms the gate covers review commands too (not just config-display ones).
Legacy config still preserved after refusal.

scenarios.title: Fresh sandbox, no pi auth — kode-review --setup with no TTY
observed: Wizard greeted: 'v1.0 runs on pi (https://pi.dev). Pi handles provider auth and model selection;'
Step 1: detected pi installed (`[OK] pi is installed`).
Step 2: detected NO usable provider; printed: `Run \`pi\` and use \`/login\` to set up a provider, then re-run kode-review.`
Wizard exited cleanly, did NOT prompt for VCS or for 'Run a code review now?' (proves wizard returned false and main() honoured the return value).
On-disk: `onboardingComplete: false` — wizard correctly did NOT mark complete.

scenarios.title: Post-migration fresh config + real diff + no pi auth — review fails with NO_PI_AUTH
scenarios.setup: Created tiny temp git repo with one staged + one unstaged change. Set onboardingComplete=true to bypass the wizard.
observed: Error: `No pi provider has usable credentials.`
Hint: `Run \`pi\` and use \`/login\` to set one up, then re-run kode-review.`
Confirms ModelRegistry-empty branch produces the AC-03 error message verbatim.

criteria_results.title: Legacy config detection and clean-break wipe

criteria.criterion: needsMigration() returns true iff legacy `provider` key present.
criteria.evidence: S3+S6 (legacy config blocks), S1 (fresh config does not) — both observable via CLI exit/behaviour. Unit-confirmed in src/cli/__tests__/migration.test.ts (`needsMigration > returns true/false`).

criteria.criterion: No-op when no legacy schema (skipReason: 'no-old-schema').
criteria.evidence: S1: fresh config → doctor runs without migration banner. Unit: `runMigration > is a no-op when no old schema is present`.

criteria.criterion: Typed confirmation must be exactly 'wipe'.
criteria.evidence: Cannot exercise interactive prompt without TTY in CLI test. Unit: `runMigration > aborts when the typed confirmation does not match "wipe"` and `> proceeds when user types exactly "wipe"`.

criteria.criterion: --migrate-yes / KODE_REVIEW_MIGRATE_YES=1 bypass the prompt.
criteria.evidence: S5: env var bypassed prompt and ran migration to completion. Unit: `runMigration > --migrate-yes / skipConfirm bypasses the prompt` and `> honors KODE_REVIEW_MIGRATE_YES env var`.

criteria.criterion: Non-TTY without override aborts with 'no-tty' (no hang).
criteria.evidence: S3+S6: stdout printed `Cannot prompt for confirmation: stdin is not a TTY.` and exited 1 immediately. Unit: `runMigration > aborts with skipReason="no-tty" when stdin is not a TTY`.

criteria.criterion: composeProject is read BEFORE resetConfig() wipes it.
criteria.evidence: S5: spinner showed `Tearing down indexer Docker project "legacy-test-project"…` — that name is the legacy-only value. Post-migration config has the default name back. Unit: `runMigration > reads composeProject from legacy config BEFORE wiping`.

criteria.criterion: Docker tear-down uses `docker compose -p <project> down -v`.
criteria.evidence: Spinner text in S5 confirms project name and tear-down intent but the literal flag list isn't echoed. Unit: `runMigration > proceeds when user types exactly "wipe"` asserts `mockExec` was called with `['compose','-p',<project>,'down','-v']`.

criteria.criterion: Migration skips docker step gracefully when docker absent.
criteria.evidence: Docker is installed on this host so the gracefulness branch isn't reached. Unit: `runMigration > skips docker tear-down when docker command is unavailable`.

criteria.criterion: Migration continues with config wipe even if docker tear-down fails.
criteria.evidence: Same as above. Unit: `runMigration > continues with config wipe even when docker tear-down fails`.

criteria.criterion: rm() targets the watch-config dir, not the main config path.
criteria.evidence: S5: post-migration `~/.config/kode-review-watch/` is gone, main config file is rewritten to defaults (NOT removed). Unit: `runMigration > proceeds when user types exactly "wipe"` asserts rm path contains `kode-review-watch`.

criteria.criterion: After resetConfig() the on-disk config has v1 schema (no legacy keys).
criteria.evidence: S5: cat'd the post-migration config — zero of `provider`, `model`, `variant`, `antigravity` keys present.

criteria.criterion: main() gates everything except --doctor on `!needsMigration()`.
criteria.evidence: S3 (--show-config blocked), S6 (--scope local blocked), S4 (--doctor allowed). All three behaviours visible in CLI output.

criteria.criterion: isPiInstalled() returns true iff `pi` is on PATH.
criteria.evidence: S7: `[OK] pi is installed` — pi is on PATH on this host. Unit: src/onboarding/__tests__/pi.test.ts.

criteria.criterion: piHasUsableModel() returns false on empty stdout / 'No models available' / non-zero exit.
criteria.evidence: S7: pi has no provider configured here, wizard correctly reported `No usable provider found in pi.` and did not advance. Unit: pi.test.ts covers all three negative branches.

criteria.criterion: Wizard returns false when pi is missing.
criteria.evidence: Pi is installed on this host. Behaviour proven indirectly by S7 (wizard short-circuits on the next missing check). Unit: covered by wizard branching in pi.test.ts.

criteria.criterion: Wizard returns false when pi has no usable model.
criteria.evidence: S7: wizard exited at step 2 without prompting for VCS or marking complete.

criteria.criterion: setOnboardingComplete(true) only fires when all checks pass.
criteria.evidence: S7: post-run config has `onboardingComplete: false`.

criteria.criterion: main() does NOT prompt 'Run a code review now?' if wizard returned false.
criteria.evidence: S7 stdout contains no such prompt; CLI exited cleanly.

criteria.criterion: Wizard text directs to `pi /login`, not a built-in OAuth.
criteria.evidence: S7 output: 'Run `pi` and use `/login` to set up a provider'.

criteria.criterion: createAgentSession is called with noTools: 'all'.
criteria.evidence: Internal option, not echoed by CLI. Unit: `runReview > disables built-in pi tools entirely for basic review (noTools = "all")`.

criteria.criterion: First available model used when --model not provided.
criteria.evidence: Requires a real pi provider; this host has none. Unit: `runReview > falls back to the first available model when no --model is set`.

criteria.criterion: --model `provider/id` matches an available model.
criteria.evidence: Unit: `runReview > passes the provided model pattern when it matches an available model`.

criteria.criterion: --model with no match throws `not available in pi`.
criteria.evidence: Unit: `runReview > throws a clear error when --model does not match any available model`.

criteria.criterion: Empty ModelRegistry → `No pi provider has usable credentials`.
criteria.evidence: S8b: end-to-end CLI emitted exactly that message and pointed user at `pi /login`.

criteria.criterion: On agent_end, last assistant message text is returned.
criteria.evidence: Requires running a real prompt. Unit: `runReview > returns the assistant text after a basic review completes` and src/review/__tests__/response.test.ts.

criteria.criterion: session.dispose() called exactly once on success / error / timeout.
criteria.evidence: Unit: `runWithPi failure paths > honors the timeout, calls session.abort(), and disposes the session` and `> surfaces session.prompt() errors and still disposes the session`.

criteria.criterion: session.prompt() rejection surfaces AND session is disposed.
criteria.evidence: Unit: `runWithPi failure paths > surfaces session.prompt() errors and still disposes the session`.

criteria.criterion: noTools: 'builtin' (NOT 'all').
criteria.evidence: Unit: `runAgenticReview > keeps built-in tools off but enables custom (extension) tools (noTools = "builtin")` includes a defensive `not.toBe('all')`.

criteria.criterion: tool_execution_end increments toolCallCount; tool_execution_start does not.
criteria.evidence: Unit: `attachReviewListener > counts each tool_execution_end event` and `> does not count tool_execution_start events`.

criteria.criterion: truncated=false / undefined when toolCallCount < maxIterations.
criteria.evidence: Unit: `runAgenticReview > counts tool executions across the session and surfaces the final text`.

criteria.criterion: truncated=true with `Maximum iteration limit` reason when toolCallCount >= maxIterations.
criteria.evidence: Unit: `runAgenticReview > marks truncated=true when tool calls hit maxIterations`.

criteria.criterion: Tool errors (isError=true) emit logger.warn including tool name; do not throw.
criteria.evidence: Unit: `attachReviewListener > emits a logger.warn when a tool completes with isError=true`.

criteria.criterion: Without indexerUrl, only read_file is registered.
criteria.evidence: Internal to the SDK. Unit: `createKodeReviewToolsExtension > always registers the read_file tool, even without an indexer URL`.

criteria.criterion: With indexerUrl, all six tools are registered.
criteria.evidence: Unit: `> registers all six tools when indexerUrl is provided`.

criteria.criterion: Every tool has non-empty label and description (>20 chars).
criteria.evidence: Unit: `> every registered tool has a non-empty description and a TypeBox parameters schema`.

criteria.criterion: parameters is a TypeBox object schema.
criteria.evidence: Same test as above.

criteria.criterion: search_code forwards (query, repoUrl, branch, 10) to IndexerClient.hybridSearch and JSON-stringifies the result.
criteria.evidence: Unit: `> search_code execute() forwards through to IndexerClient.hybridSearch and JSON-stringifies the result`.

criteria.criterion: Indexer rejection propagates to tool.execute().
criteria.evidence: Unit: `> propagates errors from indexer-backed tool handlers (does not swallow rejections)`.

criteria.criterion: Relative path inside repoRoot returns the file content.
criteria.evidence: Unit: `> read_file tool reads a file relative to the repo root through the handler`.

criteria.criterion: `../../etc/passwd` rejects with /Path traversal/.
criteria.evidence: Unit: `> read_file refuses path traversal attempts`.

criteria.criterion: Absolute paths outside repoRoot also rejected.
criteria.evidence: Not directly tested — only the `../..` form is. Recommend adding a unit case for an absolute path outside repoRoot.

criteria.criterion: Symlinks pointing outside repoRoot are rejected.
criteria.evidence: No symlink test exists. Symlink-escape is a real attack vector once the agent can drop arbitrary files; recommend adding a follow-up test.

criteria.criterion: Timeout fires with `did not complete within` message.
criteria.evidence: Unit: `runWithPi failure paths > honors the timeout, calls session.abort(), and disposes the session`.

criteria.criterion: session.abort() called exactly once on timeout.
criteria.evidence: Same test asserts `abort.toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)`.

criteria.criterion: session.dispose() called exactly once on timeout.
criteria.evidence: Same test asserts `dispose.toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)`.

criteria.criterion: If abort() rejects, timeout still propagates AND dispose() still runs.
criteria.evidence: Unit: `> still propagates the timeout error and disposes the session even when abort() rejects`.

criteria.criterion: extractReviewContent runs BEFORE session.dispose().
criteria.evidence: Verified by code structure (try { content = ... } finally { dispose() }) and by the success-path tests in engine.test.ts which read content before the test asserts dispose was called.

criteria.criterion: listener.unsubscribe() does NOT reject `done` (no UnhandledPromiseRejection).
criteria.evidence: Unit: `attachReviewListener > unsubscribe() detaches from the session and does NOT reject done`.

criteria.criterion: Fresh config validates against v1 Zod schema.
criteria.evidence: S1+S5: kode-review reads/writes the config without throwing — Zod parse runs in getConfig().

criteria.criterion: Fresh config does NOT contain `provider`/`model`/`variant`/`antigravity`.
criteria.evidence: S1+S5 cat of the on-disk config — none of those keys present.

criteria.criterion: isLegacyConfig() returns true iff `provider` key present.
criteria.evidence: S3 (legacy → blocked) and S1 (fresh → allowed) confirm the predicate matches expected gating behaviour.

criteria.criterion: resetConfig() writes fresh defaults (not empty).
criteria.evidence: S5 post-migration cat shows full default object with `indexer.composeProject='kode-review-indexer'`, full file-pattern arrays, etc.

criteria.criterion: --doctor exempt from migration gate.
criteria.evidence: S4: legacy config + --doctor → exit 0 with full diagnostic output, no migration banner.

criteria.criterion: Reports whether pi is installed.
criteria.evidence: S1+S4: `pi: Installed but no provider configured`.

criteria.criterion: Reports whether pi has usable model.
criteria.evidence: Same line as above — 'no provider configured' is the no-model branch.

criteria.criterion: Reports detected VCS CLI(s).
criteria.evidence: S1+S4: GitHub CLI status + GitLab CLI status both listed.

criteria.criterion: Doctor flags legacy config schema and points to --migrate / KODE_REVIEW_MIGRATE_YES.
criteria.evidence: S4 doctor output reads `Configuration: Valid and complete` despite the on-disk config having all four legacy keys. The diagnostic is misleading and a user inspecting --doctor on a legacy install would not learn they need to migrate. Recommend: doctor should call `hasOldSchema()` and surface a dedicated warning row referencing --migrate-yes.

criteria_results.title: show-config redacts and renders v1 schema only

criteria.criterion: show-config never prints `provider`/`model`/`variant`/`antigravity`.
criteria.evidence: S2: grep counts all zero.

criteria.criterion: No token-like values (sk-…, glpat-…, gho_…, ghp_…) printed.
criteria.evidence: S2: grep counts all zero. Plus the redesigned 'Model & Auth' section explicitly defers to pi rather than reading any tokens.

criteria.criterion: Exits 0 on a fresh/default config.
criteria.evidence: S2: exit code 0.

criteria.criterion: Legacy config + non-doctor command → migration message + non-zero exit, no pi/indexer/git work.
criteria.evidence: S3 (--show-config) and S6 (--scope local) both exit 1 with the migration banner. No git or pi calls visible in stderr; legacy state untouched.

criteria.criterion: Legacy config + --doctor → main proceeds (doctor exempt).
criteria.evidence: S4: full doctor diagnostic, exit 0.

criteria.criterion: Fresh config → main proceeds normally.
criteria.evidence: S1, S2, S7, S8b all show normal command flow with no migration banner.

criteria.criterion: Wizard return value honoured — false → no review prompt.
criteria.evidence: S7: wizard returned false (pi has no provider); CLI exited cleanly with no review prompt.

fail_details.criterion: Doctor flags legacy config schema and points to --migrate / KODE_REVIEW_MIGRATE_YES.
fail_details.observed: Doctor reports `Configuration: Valid and complete` even when the on-disk config has the legacy `provider`/`model`/`variant`/`antigravity` keys.
fail_details.recommendation: In src/cli/doctor.ts, after the existing config-validity check, call `hasOldSchema()` and emit a warning row: 'Legacy config detected — run with --migrate-yes or set KODE_REVIEW_MIGRATE_YES=1 to upgrade.'
fail_details.rationale: Doctor is the one command that bypasses the migration gate; it's also the command users run to diagnose problems. If it can't tell them they need to migrate, the gate's user-facing UX is incomplete.

gap_details.criterion: Absolute paths outside repoRoot also rejected.
gap_details.recommendation: Add a vitest case to src/review/__tests__/pi-tools.test.ts asserting `read_file.execute('id-1', { path: '/etc/passwd' })` rejects with /Path traversal/.

gap_details.criterion: Symlinks pointing outside repoRoot are rejected.
gap_details.recommendation: Add a vitest case that creates a symlink inside repoRoot pointing to an absolute path outside it, then asserts read_file rejects when given the symlink's relative path. Real defence-in-depth concern once the agent has any write capability on the host.

summary.verdict: Acceptance: PASS with 1 medium-severity recommendation (doctor should flag legacy schema) and 2 low-severity test-coverage gaps (absolute-path + symlink rejection in read_file).
